identity.canonical_title: TEMPORAL TRIGGER MECHANISM Canonical Statutory Formula: T_trig

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 1178] TEMPORAL TRIGGER MECHANISM Canonical Statutory Formula: $T_{trig} = { t_{origin} \to t_{now}, t_{retrieval} \to \text{Condition}[\text{Query}] }$ Formal Technical Dissertation: The code structure for embedding a retroactive memory. It links a current timestamp to a future conditional event, creating a "sleeping" quantum correlation that only activates (collapses) when the user queries the system in the future, completing the loop. Source: [17]
theory.technical_interpretation: TEMPORAL TRIGGER MECHANISM Canonical Statutory Formula: T_trig is an algebraic definition, written T_trig = t_origin → t_now, t_retrieval → Condition[Query]. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from t_origin, t_now, t_retrieval, defining T_trig. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
